name: MD5
definition: MD5 (Message-Digest algorithm 5) is a (now deprecated) cryptographic hash function with a 128-bit hash value used to check the integrity of files.

name: SHA-1
definition: SHA-1 (Secure Hash Algorithm-1) is a cryptographic hash function designed by the National Security Agency (NSA). It is also used to verify file integrity. Since 2011 it has been deprecated by the NIST as a U. S. government standard.

name: SHA-256
definition: SHA-256 (member of Secure Hash Algorithm-2 family) is a cryptographic hash function designed by the National Security Agency (NSA) and published by the NIST as a U. S. government standard. It is also used to verify file integrity.
